claims.text: In December 1990, two Russian scientists (Vlail Kaznacheev and Alexander Trofimov) ran an ESP-amplification experiment in Dixon, the northernmost settlement in Asia, using rolled-aluminum 'Kozyrev mirror' chambers — and the device produced apparent psychic powers, time-traveling visions, and accompanying UFO sightings, all documented in their book Cosmic Consciousness of Humanity.
entities: location:in a remote village above the arctic circle
organization:the institute of experimental medicine of the academy of sciences siberian branch

evidence.quote:

above the Arctic Circle two Russian

human super perception or ESP they built

their biological energy the device was a

the device worked but maybe it worked a

world and soon they could view any place
in time in fact these experiments
confirmed the theory first proposed in
the 1950s the time as we know it
doesn't exist

claims.rationale: Vlail Kaznacheev and Alexander Trofimov are real Russian scientists who did run a series of 'Kozyrev mirror' parapsychology experiments through the Institute of Clinical and Experimental Medicine, Siberian Branch (Novosibirsk), and they did publish a book ('Cosmic Consciousness of Humanity') describing the results. The experimental claims (telepathy, time visions, UFOs over the lab) are real claims they made; they have not been replicated by independent labs and are not accepted by mainstream physics or parapsychology. So the historical existence of the experiments is true; the experimental results are extraordinarily contested.

claims.text: Nikolai Kozyrev was a real and intermittently vindicated 20th-century Russian astrophysicist whose 1958 prediction of lunar volcanism was confirmed by later Apollo data, lending credibility to his subsequent and far more controversial claims about the nature of time.

evidence.quote:
as far as scientists go Nikolai kazarev
isn't exactly a household name but in
the early 20th century he was a
prominent Russian physicist whose
Innovative and controversial theories
are still debated today kazarev made
discoveries in astrophysics that were at
first rejected but later proven true in
1958 he reported volcanic activity on
the moon at first his claim was
dismissed by the scientific Community
later the Apollo lunar missions proved
kazareth was right
what moon landing Landing fake well the
volcanism was photographed by unmanned
missions to the Moon okay but later
confirmed when astronauts brought moon
rocks back to Earth

claims.rationale: Nikolai Kozyrev (1908-1983) is a real Soviet astrophysicist. He did report a transient lunar event (a reddish glow at the crater Alphonsus) in November 1958 using spectroscopy at the Crimean Observatory, and the observation was at first dismissed by Western lunar scientists but later considered evidence of lunar outgassing/transient lunar phenomena, partially vindicated by Apollo-era thermal data. His broader 'time energy' / torsion theory was never accepted by mainstream physics. The host's framing of the lunar-volcanism vindication is essentially accurate.

claims.text: Kozyrev's 'time as a physical force' theory holds that time is an active substance carrying energy, transmitted instantaneously through a luminiferous ether, twisting space-time via torsion fields and influencing the spin of galaxies, weather, DNA, and human consciousness — directly contradicting mainstream relativistic physics, which holds time is a passive dimension and that the ether does not exist.

heartbeat of the universe it creates
energy that impacts matter and space and

the same time energy influences

the people who live on it time energy

orbis of planets time energy feeds every

claims.rationale: The host accurately summarizes Kozyrev's published theory of 'causal mechanics' / 'time energy', but the theory is not accepted by mainstream physics. The Michelson-Morley experiment and subsequent special relativity tests rule out a luminiferous ether to extraordinary precision; general relativity treats time as a coordinate and tests of FTL information transfer have all failed. Kozyrev's torsion claims are pseudoscience as a description of reality, even though he was a real astrophysicist with one genuine vindication (lunar volcanism). The 'host accurately reports the theory' part is fine; the 'theory is true' part fails physics.

claims.text: Kozyrev claimed empirical proof of his theory by detecting torsion signatures from a star at three positions simultaneously: where its light is currently arriving from (past), where it actually is now (faster than light), and where it will be in the future — implying time-energy moves instantaneously and all moments coexist.

get here fine cozarev detected torsion
from that star ten thousand light years
away so that torsion was also from the
past next kosarev calculated where the
star was at the current time he detected
torsion there too in fact the reading

instantaneous kosarev went a step
further he calculated where the star
would be in the distant future he
detected torsion there too Kaiser have
concluded that all time is simultaneous
and infinite that past and future are
just metaphors

claims.rationale: Kozyrev did report telescope-pendulum experiments in the 1970s claiming to detect 'true positions' of stars (i.e. faster-than-light signals). Lavrentiev and others later attempted replications. Mainstream review (e.g. Levich's reviews, plus Western analyses) consistently identifies thermal drift, gravitational artifacts, and statistical issues with the protocols. No mainstream physics journal treats these results as detection of FTL information. Host accurately reports Kozyrev's claim; the underlying physics is not supported.

dependencies.rationale: Cited as the experimental backing for the torsion theory.

claims.text: Kozyrev built and patented a Fibonacci-spiraled aluminum 'time mirror' that he claimed could focus time-energy and let him see ten seconds into the future at the microscopic level — work the Soviet government classified as a state secret, and he died on February 27, 1983 just before testing the larger version.

new machine oh no Nikolai kazarev

claims.rationale: Kozyrev did die in 1983 and did publish on aluminum concave-mirror experiments. The 'patent' claim is murkier — Kazanchev/Trofimov hold patents on mirror designs in Russia, attributed to Kozyrev's framework. The 'see 10 seconds into the future' specific claim comes from the host's own framing of Kozyrev's prediction-experiment results, not a verifiable demonstrated capability. The 'mysteriously died' framing is the host's editorializing — he died of natural causes at age 74. Mix of accurate biographical fact and conspiratorially-framed embellishment.

claims.text: The CIA's Project Stargate (1972-1995) and parallel Soviet psychic-research programs both pursued remote viewing as intelligence assets, with the Soviet program — including the Kozyrev mirror experiments — reportedly more 'successful' than the American counterpart, per declassified CIA documents.

evidence.quote:
the most famous American program was
Project Stargate this program explored
various psychic abilities but
specifically concentrated on remote
viewing which is trying to visualize a
distant Target using only the mind now
the results of project Stargate were
mixed but there were a few reported
successes the Soviet Union also engaged
in psychic Espionage and they invested
heavily in parapsychology and psychic
phenomena and the Soviets according to
Declassified CIA documents seem to have
been more successful in their attempts

claims.rationale: Project Stargate is real (DIA/CIA-funded SRI program, 1972-1995, terminated after AIR review found no actionable intelligence). The Soviets did invest in parapsychology, and CIA documents on the Kazanchev/Trofimov work were declassified in the early 2000s. Whether the Soviet program was 'more successful' is a propagandistic claim — the CIA documents describe the Soviet claims, not endorse their accuracy. The historical existence of both programs is solid; the comparative-success framing leans on Soviet self-assessment.

claims.text: In the Kazanchev/Trofimov experiments, two Kozyrev-mirror operators in different cities mentally broadcast images, and 5,000 participants in 12 countries received the symbols — with success rates correlating with Earth's magnetospheric activity and jumping to 95% during solar storms.

operator sits in a causative mirror at
the lab in Dixon another person sits in
a mirror at the town of novosibirsk 1400
miles away the operator is then told to
concentrate on a symbol and send that
symbol to the receiver in novosibirsk

influence many processes on Earth from

two operators and two different kazarev

and view the symbol being sent when the

who had been watching closely

claims.rationale: Kazanchev and Trofimov did publish these multi-site telepathy/symbol-transmission claims in their own monographs, with the magnetospheric correlations and 95% peak figures appearing in their reports. No independent replication exists, the participant pool selection and statistical methodology have not been peer-reviewed, and parapsychology has a 50+ year history of methodological problems even at less spectacular effect sizes. The host accurately reports the Russian researchers' claims; the underlying empirical accuracy is unsupported.

claims.text: Subjects inside Kozyrev mirrors consistently reported encountering humanoid 'Observers' made of light who could communicate, physically touch the subjects, and on at least one occasion warned that 'your planet is in danger.'

claims.rationale: The 'Observers' transcripts are real in the sense that Kazanchev and Trofimov's book documents subject reports of these encounters. They are first-person psychological reports under sensory-deprivation-like conditions (sitting in a small reflective metal chamber for hours), which is exactly the kind of setup known in psychology to produce hallucinations, hypnagogic imagery, and religious-experience analogues. The reports are real; the supernatural interpretation is unsupported.

claims.text: Over 40% of Kozyrev-mirror subjects reported time-travel experiences ranging from witnessing their own younger lives to inhabiting historical figures (a Genghis Khan advisor, someone in the medieval period, someone in the Roman Empire).

life like watching a movie others were
actually able to interact with and

remember Nikolai kosarov believed that

claims.rationale: Like the Observer encounters, these are subject self-reports from inside the mirror chambers, faithfully transcribed in the Russian researchers' books. They are not externally corroborated time travel; they are vivid hypnagogic imagery in a sensory-altered state, which is consistent with the well-known psychology of isolation chambers. The reports happened; the time-travel interpretation is not supported.

claims.text: A 2023 City University of New York experiment at the Advanced Science Research Center demonstrated 'time reflection' of electromagnetic waves through a metamaterial, partially vindicating Kozyrev's claim that time is non-linear and reflectable.

evidence.quote:
until last month scientists at the
advanced science research center at the
City University of New York conducted an
experiment they sent an electromagnetic
wave through a meta material these waves
also have a Time component that can be
measured the wave went in moving forward
and came out moving backward this is
what's called a Time reflection and this
was pure Theory until now so Nikolai
kazarev was right time isn't as linear
as we thought

claims.rationale: The CUNY ASRC group (Andrea Alù, Hady Moussa et al.) did publish in Nature Physics 2023 demonstrating electromagnetic time reflections in a metamaterial — the result is real. However, 'time reflection' in the metamaterial sense (a rapid change in the medium's properties causing wave reflection in the time domain) is a wave-propagation phenomenon, not a vindication of Kozyrev's metaphysics about time as a physical substance. The host conflates a real and interesting metamaterial result with Kozyrev's much grander claim. Real experiment, overreaching interpretation.

claims.text: Trofimov claims that his Kozyrev-mirror-based earthquake prediction reaches 61% accuracy, vastly outperforming the ~8% accuracy that conventional geological prediction achieves.

evidence.quote:
Dr travamov has been
using kosarev's theories and causer of
mirrors to predict earthquakes again
sounds like science fiction but
geologist's accuracy in earthquake
prediction is on average eight percent
in 2018 travamov's accuracy was as high
as 61 percent

claims.rationale: Trofimov has indeed published these prediction-rate claims (in Russian-language journals). Earthquake prediction is famously hard — neither geophysics nor ML has reached useful prediction skill, and the USGS official position is that no such method works. Trofimov's 61% claim has not been independently verified, blinded, or replicated. The 8% baseline is also a fudged comparator. As with most other claims in this video, Trofimov really did say this; the underlying empirical claim is implausible.

claims.text: The Kozyrev-mirror story occupies an unresolved middle ground: mainstream science ignores it, the CIA quietly tracks it, the inventors still publish, and even with one document on the technology's potential remaining classified, anyone can buy the blueprints and build their own — making this a thesis worth investigating rather than dismissing.

evidence.quote:
whether you
believe in the cause of mirror or not
comes down to do you believe Nikolai
kazarev's theory about time energy and
The Ether mainstream science doesn't
believe in anything called The Ether
but if instead of ether kosarev called
it dark matter or quantum foam or
emergent space maybe more people would
have accepted his ideas because of head
of few ideas rejected by mainstream
science that were later proven to be
true what if he's right about this too

claims.rationale: This is the host's editorial verdict — a sympathetic reading. The factual claims it makes (CIA does have declassified documents, inventors do still publish, blueprints exist online) are true. The persuasive overlay (Kozyrev was vindicated once on lunar volcanism, so we should give his time theory the benefit of the doubt) is rhetorical leverage on a one-time success. I read this as a sympathetic but speculative editorial.
